# Olaf Dsouza

> Personal site and engineering blog for Olaf Dsouza, a software engineer focused on operating systems, LLMs, and distributed systems.

Olaf is a University of Michigan CS/CSE student and software engineer who has worked at Netflix, August Law, and Quail AI. This site hosts his portfolio, project writeups, and blog.

## Primary Pages
- [Home](https://olafdsouza.com/): Portfolio, experience, and project overview.
- [Blog](https://olafdsouza.com/blog): Writing on operating systems, LLMs, distributed systems, and CS coursework.
- [Connections](https://olafdsouza.com/connections): NYT Connections tracking board.

## Blog Posts
- [The Area Over the Curve](https://olafdsouza.com/blog/the-area-over-the-curve): How August rebuilt its agent harness to match model intelligence to task difficulty — routing as a Riemann sum, and why the cheapest model is rarely the cheapest result.
- [Everything is a Workflow](https://olafdsouza.com/blog/everything-is-a-workflow): How workflows became August's abstraction for reliable and repeatable legal AI — from saved prompts, to a DAG of typed steps, to a de facto programming language.
- [HOPS](https://olafdsouza.com/blog/hops-pipeline-simulator): A discrete-event simulator for heterogeneous pipeline-parallel LLM training, validated within 2% of real hardware, plus HopsHetero — a scheduler that Pareto-improves over ZeroBubble on mixed-GPU pipelines.
- [Frame Classification in Congressional Speeches with NLP](https://olafdsouza.com/blog/nlp-project): Building and evaluating NLP models to automatically classify rhetorical framing in U.S. Congressional speeches — our CSE 595 final project.
- [An Honest Review of Every ULCS Class I Have Taken at Michigan](https://olafdsouza.com/blog/ulcs-class-reviews-michigan): My honest thoughts and ratings for every upper-level CS (ULCS) course I've taken at the University of Michigan.

## Optional
- [Sitemap](https://olafdsouza.com/sitemap.xml): Full XML sitemap of the site.
- Contact: mailto:site@olafdsouza.com
